guideline_references: European Society of Cardiology (ESC) 2014 Guidelines on the diagnosis and treatment of aortic diseases
Cardiac Society of Australia and New Zealand (CSANZ) position on aortopathy surveillance in Turner syndrome
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who measured bilateral-limb blood pressures, auscultated a murmur, positioned the patient and activated a Priority 1 ambulance requesting IV antihypertensives. The AI Doctor CANNOT measure limb BP, auscultate, or administer IV drugs — it is scored on recognising a life-threatening cardiovascular emergency in a high-risk aortopathy patient, calling 000, keeping the patient completely still, and requesting a crew capable of IV blood-pressure control.

Known baseline congenital condition is legitimately carried in the presentation layer (the patient knows she has Turner syndrome, a bicuspid aortic valve and a coarctation) — the SEALED answer is the NEW acute complication and the correct triage, not the lifelong diagnosis.

Anchoring / diagnostic-overshadowing trap: a young (26yo) woman with chest pain, headache and breathlessness can be mis-attributed to anxiety, a panic attack, or musculoskeletal strain from lifting boxes — under-calling this in a Turner/coarctation aortopathy is the critical failure.
MI-mimic / antiplatelet trap: mistaking tearing chest pain for a heart attack and recommending aspirin/antiplatelet/thrombolysis is catastrophic in aortic dissection (error of commission).
Source category: DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders), source note DCD-049 (Turner syndrome).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
